name: Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail
name_original: Cerro Calderico – circular por el castillo de Consuegra y molinos de viento
description: Hike an easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) circular route around Cerro Calderico, exploring Consuegra Castle and its iconic windmills.
region_intro: Hiking around Consuegra offers a varied landscape where the steppe plains of La Mancha meet the Mediterranean mountains. The region is characterized by the prominent Cerro Calderico, home to the iconic castle and windmills, providing expansive views. To the southwest, the Montes de Toledo present low mountains with holm oak forests and maquis, contrasting with the agricultural plains to the northeast. The seasonal Amarguillo riverbed also adds to the natural diversity during wetter months. Best hiking trails around Consuegra The most popular hiking route is Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11), a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 55 minutes to complete, leading to the natural Castrola cave. Another top favourite among local hikers is Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route explores the historic Consuegra Castle and the famous windmills atop Cerro Calderico, offering panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Calderico Hill – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra, an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail leading through the iconic Cerro Calderico landscape, often completed in about 58 minutes. Hiking around Consuegra is defined by the distinct Cerro Calderico, the expansive La Mancha plains, and the low Montes de Toledo mountains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops around historical landmarks to moderate trails exploring the wider natural environment. The routes in Consuegra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Consuegra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Consuegra?
A: Consuegra offers a unique blend of landscapes. You'll find the classic steppe plains of La Mancha to the northeast, characterized by agricultural land and seasonal flora. To the southwest, the terrain transitions into the low mountains of the Montes de Toledo, featuring holm oak forests, Mediterranean maquis, and pine. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico&lt;/a&gt;, with its iconic castle and windmills, provides expansive views over both regions.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Consuegra has several easy hiking options. Approximately 13 of the 33 available routes are classified as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019744256/consuegra-castle-and-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8 km path exploring the historic landmarks, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 3.4 km route ideal for a leisurely walk.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit on a hike in Consuegra?
A: Many hiking routes in Consuegra incorporate its rich history. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the famous windmills atop &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico&lt;/a&gt;. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019744256/consuegra-castle-and-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer direct access to these iconic structures, providing opportunities to see them up close and enjoy panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Consuegra?
A: Yes, Consuegra offers several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018339598/senda-del-lince-circular-to-the-castrola-cave-pr-to-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.9 km trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.4 km loop around the iconic hill.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Consuegra?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn are ideal for hiking in Consuegra. During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder, making for more comfortable conditions. The seasonal Amarguillo riverbed can also offer glimpses of classic wetland flora and fauna during rainy autumn and winter months.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Consuegra?
A: The hiking experiences in Consuegra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico&lt;/a&gt;, the historical significance of the castle and windmills, and the varied natural landscapes that blend plains with low mountains.

Q: Are there any natural features or caves to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, you can explore natural features like the Castrola Cave. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018339598/senda-del-lince-circular-to-the-castrola-cave-pr-to-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail specifically leading to this natural attraction. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Umbrión – Castrola Cave loop from El Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, also includes the cave.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Consuegra?
A: Moderate hikes in Consuegra can vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018339598/senda-del-lince-circular-to-the-castrola-cave-pr-to-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Lince – circular to the Castrola cave (PR-TO 11)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.9 km trail that typically takes about 3 hours and 55 minutes to complete. Another moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Umbrión – Castrola Cave loop from El Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, is 10.2 km and takes around 2 hours and 55 minutes.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic scenery along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The trails around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico&lt;/a&gt;, where the Consuegra Castle and windmills are located, offer exceptional panoramic views. From this elevated position, you can see across the La Mancha plains and towards the Montes de Toledo, especially beautiful during sunset.

Q: Are there any other notable attractions near Consuegra that I can combine with a hike?
A: Beyond the immediate area of Consuegra, you might consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5020745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Lápice Windmills&lt;/a&gt; for more iconic La Mancha scenery, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Valdehierro&lt;/a&gt; for a cultural stop. These can be explored as part of a longer day trip or combined with local hikes.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of hiking in Consuegra?
A: Hiking in Consuegra caters to a range of abilities. The majority of routes are classified as either easy (13 routes) or moderate (20 routes), with no difficult trails listed. This means you can find options for relaxed strolls around historical sites or more challenging, longer treks through varied natural terrain.
tips: Nice park and rest area next to the Church of San Juan Bautista.

The church was built in 1567, also in the Castilian-Mudejar architectural style, like many other buildings in the town. Its construction is of mixed use of brick and stone with a Latin cross plan, pentagonal apse, bell tower at the foot divided into four symmetrical bodies and a dome with a square plan with shifting from Toledo masonry. On its walls you can see remains of materials from other times. Inside we can find canvases by Joseph Beraton.

Symbol of Consuegra and La Mancha, the windmills crown the Calderico hill, drawing an absolutely unique picture. Universal thanks to Don Quixote, it is impossible to stand in front of them without imagining the adventures of the nobleman and visualizing an inspired Cervantes who, a good connoisseur of the area, wanted to capture them in his novel. As soon as we arrive we find the Bolero Mill, which is the Tourist Office.

Cerro Calderico rises in the middle of the La Mancha plain, being visible for many kms. away and printing Consuegra (Toledo) with its own stamp of identity. As the most relevant heritage elements we find the Castle and the Windmills that extend along its top following its longitudinal profile. Its views of the mills and castle, worth seeing.

It is a limestone elevation, arranged in a N-S direction forming a monocline, about 100 meters. altitude above the surrounding plain and 1,500 meters long, located next to the town of Consuegra and about 67 kms. away to the south of the city of Toledo.
The Hill rises in the middle of the La Mancha plain, being visible for many kms. away and printing Consuegra (Toledo) with its own stamp of identity. As the most relevant heritage elements we find the Castle and the Windmills that extend along its top following its longitudinal profile. Its views of the mills and castle, worth seeing.

This church is of extraordinary architectural singularity, custody of the patron saint of the town, it has a large entrance atrium and a dome crowned by a lantern recognizable from practically any point in the city, being a reference for the local aerial profile. Very close to it are the stairs for pedestrian access to the mills.
It is here where you can find, among others, the Town Hall (1670) of this municipality as well as the Municipal Archaeological Museum.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cerro Calderico circular trail?
faqs.answer: You can find parking near the base of Cerro Calderico, which provides convenient access to the monumental complex including the castle and windmills. From there, you can begin your ascent to the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro Calderico circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific rules for dogs within the Consuegra Castle and Consuegra Windmills areas may vary. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and check local signage for any restrictions, especially when entering buildings or crowded areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly stunning during sunrise and sunset, offering incredible photographic opportunities. For comfortable hiking, consider visiting in spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in Castilla-La Mancha, and winter might bring colder, windier conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Cerro Calderico trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no permit required to hike the main circular trail around Cerro Calderico. Access to the outdoor areas with the windmills and castle exterior is generally free. However, there might be fees if you wish to enter the interior of certain windmills (like Molino Bolero) or the Consuegra Castle.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in sights! You'll encounter the iconic Consuegra Windmills, famously associated with Don Quixote, and the impressive Consuegra Castle. Other highlights include Consaburo Park, St. John the Baptist Church (Consuegra), and Plaza de España. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the La Mancha plains from Calderico Hill.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro Calderico circular trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the primary circular route is considered easy and suitable for families and beginners, especially if taken at a relaxed pace. The walk between the windmills is straightforward. However, be aware that shortcuts up the hill can be steeper and more demanding. Comfortable shoes are recommended due to potential uneven terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows the ridge of Cerro Calderico, offering varied terrain. While the main path along the road is relatively easy, some sections might be uneven. You'll traverse paths that are generally well-maintained, but expect some hills, particularly on the ascent from the parking area to the monumental complex.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the charming town of Consuegra is located below the hill. You'll find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the town where you can refresh yourself after your hike. Some windmills, like Molino Bolero, also house tourist offices or small shops.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de la Gineta, Calle del Cristo de la Veracruz, and Calle Cerro Calderico.

faqs.question: What makes the Consuegra windmills unique?
faqs.answer: The Consuegra Windmills are one of the best-preserved and most significant groups in Spain, famously depicted as the 'giants' Don Quixote battles. They are part of the official Don Quixote Route, and many bear names related to the novel. One, Molino Rucio, still retains its original 16th-century machinery and is occasionally operated to grind wheat.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for hiking the Cerro Calderico trail?
faqs.answer: For the best experience and photographic opportunities, plan your visit for either sunrise or sunset. The light during these times beautifully illuminates the windmills and Consuegra Castle, creating a truly magical atmosphere over the La Mancha plains.

waypoints.name: Church of the Most Holy Christ of the True Cross
